key_insight: DT-SegNet combines object detection and segmentation to automatically identify precipitates in electron microscopy images with higher accuracy than traditional threshold-based methods.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more reliable measurement of precipitate area and distribution, supporting microstructure optimization in chromium-based superalloys for high-temperature applications.
method: Stage 1: YOLOv5 detects precipitate regions in electron microscopy images.
Stage 2: SegFormer refines the detected regions with high-accuracy segmentation.
End-to-end pipeline improves automation for precipitate area and volume-fraction analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that DT-SegNet outperforms Weka and ilastik across accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score in electron microscopy image evaluation.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of training images, so cross-material generalization should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Accurate identification and measurement of the precipitate area by two-stage deep neural networks in novel chromium-based alloys
